questions.question: Which CSS property allows you to define a non-rectangular shape that inline content wraps around?
questions.explanation: The shape-outside property defines a shape around which inline content will wrap. It only works on floated elements.

questions.question: Which shape function creates a circular wrapping boundary with a 50% radius centered in the element?
questions.explanation: The circle() function accepts a radius parameter. circle(50%) creates a circle with a radius of 50% of the element's dimensions.

questions.question: What does the shape-margin property do?
choices: Sets the margin of the floated element
Adds space between the shape boundary and the wrapping content
Defines the inner padding of the shape
Sets the distance between two shaped elements
questions.explanation: The shape-margin property adds a margin around the defined shape, creating extra space between the shape boundary and the inline content that wraps around it.

questions.question: Which shape function would you use to define an irregular wrapping boundary with multiple vertices?
questions.explanation: The polygon() function accepts a comma-separated list of coordinate pairs, allowing you to define complex irregular shapes with multiple vertices.

questions.question: The ___ property controls the alpha channel threshold when using an image to define a float shape.
questions.explanation: The shape-image-threshold property sets the alpha value threshold for extracting a shape from an image. Pixels above the threshold contribute to the shape.

questions.question: The inset() shape function creates a ___ shape inset from the edges of the element's reference box.
questions.explanation: The inset() function defines a rectangular shape by specifying offsets from each edge of the reference box, optionally with border-radius for rounded corners.
